title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and state should be anchored in session logs rather than in orchestration layers, tools, or LLMs. As models commoditize, durable append-only logs become the key differentiator, enabling durability, continuity, forkability, and portability across different runtimes and model providers.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara makes the case that an agent's identity and state belong in session logs, not in orchestration layers, tools, or the LLMs themselves. The argument: as models commoditize, the durable append-only log becomes the real differentiator. The concrete payoffs are durability, continuity, forkability, and portability, letting agent state move across different runtimes and model providers.
